content.claim: Church-run institutions now account for approximately 20% of all schools and over 33% of secondary schools in Hungary, with documented patterns of preferential state funding, attraction of higher-qualified teachers, and admissions discretion enabling selection of higher-achieving students, alongside reported exodus of non-Roma children from state schools to church institutions.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: 'Hollowing out' framing is evaluative language, though patterns are documented
Exact proportion of state funding advantage to church schools not specified in retrieved notes
Causality of non-Roma exodus attribution relies on reported patterns rather than quantified administrative data
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
